title: Multiple status columns in one chart and defining the order that chart bars appear
excerpt:

I have 2 Status columns that I want to represent on the same chart, but I can’t find a way of doing this. Each item/row has a 2 RAG status (Red/Amber/Green) columns and I want the number of Red, Amb…

posts.author: neilpenry (Neil Penry)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have 2 questions:

  1. I have 2 Status columns that I want to represent on the same chart, but I can’t find a way of doing this. Each item/row has a 2 RAG status (Red/Amber/Green) columns and I want the number of Red, Amber and Green items from both columns, is it possible?
  2. I want to change the order in which the bars are represented on the chart, Green, then Amber, then Red, but is seems to put them in alphabetical order. I want them in the order that they appear in the drop down list of statuses in the table. Can this be done?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Neil,

  1. At this time it is only possible to select one status column per board via the chart view - that said, could you consider using the stacked bar chart? This way you can select the two columns to be reported on. Let me know your thoughts!

  2. I am afraid it isn’t currently possible to further customise the order of the bars - sorry for the inconvenience here! I did want to note however that the team is aware that the limitation exists are are considering addressing in the future

posts.author: neilpenry (Neil Penry)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s a wider issue I think, we also need to be able to add several number columns to a chart (eg a pie chart showing budget, how much spent and budget remaining for example) or amount of cost before and after an event

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
I definitely understand where you’re coming from here!

Please go ahead and submit this as a feature request so our community can vote, and increase the likelihood of this being rolled out.
